Six Days · Rajasthan, India · December 2026

Bundi,
Rajasthan

A crumbling fort above a blue city that Kipling wrote about and Instagram hasn't found yet — rent a haveli, own the place, and feel like you're somewhere genuinely rare.

The Vibe

There are cities that perform for visitors and cities that simply exist. Bundi does the latter. Blue-washed houses climb the hillside in overlapping layers, a crumbling palace rises above them, and a 14th-century fort crowns the whole thing like a punctuation mark. Stepwells appear around corners with no signage. Monkeys occupy the rooftops without apology. The tourists who do come tend to leave quietly and come back.

Where You'll Stay

The Haveli Braj Bhushanjee has been in the same family for 200 years — the same family that produced four of Bundi's Prime Ministers in the 19th century. It sits on the road to the palace, which means the fort is visible from the rooftop at every hour, and illuminated at night in a way that makes it difficult to go to bed. Several rooms carry original Bundi School murals on the walls. The Darikhana dining room, where the family has fed travellers for generations, serves food that is simple, vegetarian, and quietly exceptional.

Six Days in Rajasthan

Day One · Sat 5 Dec — Jaipur

The Pink City

Jaipur is the warm-up act. Arrive when you can — the old city will absorb you immediately. Hawa Mahal turns a particular shade of amber as the sun drops behind it. The Johari Bazaar is all jewellery and noise and smell and colour. Dinner together that evening, the group assembling around a table, the trip properly beginning.

Day Two · Sun 6 Dec — The Drive

Into the Countryside

The drive from Jaipur takes the better part of a morning. Scrubland gives way to rock formations, small villages appear and disappear, the landscape grows quieter and stranger as the road descends. Then the blue city resolves itself from the hillside — the palace above it, the fort higher still. The haveli receives you. Claim a room, find the rooftop, order chai. The afternoon is yours.

Day Three · Mon 7 Dec — The Palace

Garh Palace and Taragarh Fort

The Badal Mahal — the Cloud Palace — is the reason to come to Bundi. A room of floor-to-ceiling murals painted between 1687 and 1707, vivid blue-greens and ochres depicting Krishna's court, hunting scenes, celestial armies. They have been here for three centuries and they look it, and that is entirely the point. A guide is not optional. The Chitrashala gallery and Hall of Public Audience follow. Then the climb to Taragarh Fort above — largely unrestored, largely empty, the entire city spread below you in its blue improbability.

Day Four · Tue 8 Dec — Stepwells & River

The City of Stepwells

Raniji ki Baori was built in 1699 and descends 150 feet through tiers of carved sandstone — a feat of engineering that also happens to be one of the most visually arresting structures in India. Bundi has fifty of these scattered through its lanes, most unmarked, most unvisited. A local guide finds them. The Chambal River in the afternoon: narrow gorges, marsh birds in extraordinary numbers, gharial crocodiles motionless on sandbars in a way that takes a moment to register.

Day Five · Wed 9 Dec — Open Day

Slow Bundi

The morning takes you out to Garardha, where prehistoric rock paintings sit exposed on open hillside — thousands of years old, no barriers, almost no visitors. Back by noon. The afternoon is genuinely free: the silver jewellery quarter, a miniature painting studio where you can watch an artist work at a scale that requires a magnifying glass, or nothing at all. Nawal Sagar Lake at golden hour, where the half-submerged temple of Varuna sits in still water and the whole city arranges itself around it for the last time.

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Sukh Mahal, Then Chennai

Sukh Mahal is a small pleasure palace on the edge of the lake — the place where Kipling stayed and found the material for Kim. It takes thirty minutes and leaves an impression disproportionate to its size. Then the road back to Jaipur, the flight south at 17:25, and Chennai by eight in the evening. Check in, settle in. The wedding starts tomorrow.

Also Worth Knowing

Getting There

Plan to arrive at Jaipur Airport on Saturday 5 December — flights connect from most major Asian cities. The group spends one night together in Jaipur, then drives to Bundi the following morning — 3.5 hours through open Rajasthan countryside.

The Flight to Chennai

Jaipur → Chennai departs 17:25 on Thu 10 Dec, arrives 20:00. Drive back to Jaipur airport by early afternoon — comfortable timing after a morning at Sukh Mahal.

Why December in Rajasthan

Rajasthan in December runs at 25°C by day and cool enough for a layer by night. The post-monsoon green hasn't faded yet, the afternoon light is long and golden, and the crowds that clog Jaipur and Udaipur thin considerably by the time you reach Bundi. It is, without much competition, the best month to be here. The Bundi Utsav festival sometimes falls in this window — worth checking if it overlaps.

Food Highlights

Dal baati churma — wheat dumplings fired in wood coals, cracked open and served with spiced lentils and a sweet crumble of roasted flour — is the dish that defines this part of Rajasthan. The Darikhana at the haveli is the place to eat it. Street chai in Bundi is a serious thing: thick, spiced, served in small clay cups. The ladoo known as bundi — the small fried droplets of chickpea batter — takes its name from this city. Eat them from the market, not from a hotel buffet.

The Full Trip at a Glance

The Side Trip

Sat 5 Dec

Arrive Jaipur. One night in the Pink City together.

Sun 6 – Wed 9 Dec

Four nights in Bundi. Forts, stepwells, river safaris, open days.

Thu 10 Dec

Morning at Sukh Mahal. Fly Jaipur → Chennai 17:25, arrive 20:00.

The Wedding

Thu 10 Dec — Evening

Arrive Chennai. Check in, settle in.

Fri 11 – Sun 13 Dec

Wedding festivities across three days in Chennai.

Mon 14 Dec

Depart Chennai. Fly home.

Covers all accommodation, meals, ground transport, activities and guided tours from the moment you land in Jaipur through to the flight to Chennai. Flights to and from Jaipur are not included.

Everyone goes to Jaipur. The people who've been to Bundi don't tell you about it — they just go back.
